text.title: Trend Research Gotchas
text.summary: Critical failure modes in trend research: training-cutoff bias, hallucinated cross-functional claims, SEO-ranked results, vendor copy, human-in-the-loop gates.

antipattern.description: Accepting trend assertions without a 2025+ URL, resulting in 2023-era practices presented as 2026 trends.
antipattern.reason: LLM training data is structurally behind real markets. "OKRs revival" and "design sprints" were popular in 2023 and will confidently appear as 2026 trends unless a hard recency gate is enforced.

antipattern.description: Over-indexing on US and San Francisco Bay Area sources without requiring EU or APAC primary sources.
antipattern.reason: Most LLM training data and most tech media is US-centric. A trend that is maturing in SF may be early or absent in EU/APAC markets. Require at least one EU and one APAC source per trend axis.

antipattern.description: Accepting "cross-functional teams" or "rapid pivots, quarterly cycles" as findings without a concrete artifact (org chart, job posting, public retro, case study).
antipattern.reason: These are popular LLM completions that recombine from training data regardless of evidence. Require a named team or published case study; reject cadence claims without a specific organisation and source.

antipattern.description: Letting three citations to the same secondary article inflate confidence for a claim.
antipattern.reason: HBR, McKinsey blog, and Forbes often cite each other. Three links to the same underlying source count as one evidence point, not three.

antipattern.description: Using raw WebSearch results ranked by SEO rather than by publication date.
antipattern.reason: SEO-optimised blog spam ranks above primary research. Sort by published date in the scraper and down-rank domains on a vendor-blog denylist before summarising.

rule.statement: Prefer primary sources (Linear's public changelog, PostHog's product blog, Dovetail's annual research reports) over secondary aggregators for trend evidence.
rule.rationale: Primary sources reflect real practice at named organisations; secondary aggregators amplify whatever is fashionable regardless of whether it ships outcomes.

antipattern.description: Conflating product-development-trends-2026 with product-development-trends (the non-year-pinned sibling methodology).
antipattern.reason: Two subagents running simultaneously on overlapping prompts produce two reports that a merge agent will silently resolve by choosing one, discarding the other.

rule.statement: Cap the token cost of the synthesis stage by pre-filtering signals with the scoring script before invoking Opus-class synthesis.
rule.rationale: Synthesis on 50+ unfiltered signals on Opus can exceed 100k tokens. The scoring script reduces the input to the 8-12 strongest signals, keeping synthesis cost proportionate to the value delivered.

list.item: After Pass 1 JSON spec: human confirms the 4-6 trend axes are the right ones for this product context before Pass 2 sweeps begin.
Before writing into .aidocs/product_docs/: human accepts the final scored signal table. Hallucinated tool or vendor names must not land in deliverables.
Before merging trend report into roadmap: human signs off on framing. This document shapes roadmap conversations.
